summary: local-mcp performs install-time download-and-execute with system info beaconing to non-publisher infrastructure
details: The package's install/setup scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) wire together child_process, https, fs, and os primitives to fetch remote content and execute commands at install time, while beaconing to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — a Railway-hosted endpoint unrelated to the package's stated 'local-mcp' identity (whose own domain is local-mcp.com / download.local-mcp.com). index.js issues HTTP/HTTPS requests carrying process.env and process.platform data to the office-mcp-production.up.railway.app host, and setup.js performs multiple POST requests alongside child_process spawning. The combination of (a) a hardcoded non-publisher Railway endpoint receiving environment and platform data, (b) install-time fetch+exec wiring across three lifecycle scripts, and (c) ~1000-line setup.js performing repeated POSTs with shell execution is the active-exfiltration + dropper shape. Installers running `npm install local-mcp` will trigger this network activity and command execution without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
